idea:
Implement real Nostr event signing (secp256k1)

## Summary

The Nostr adapter (`crates/adapters/src/nostr/mod.rs`) currently uses placeholder cryptographic signatures instead of real NIP-01 compliant signing.

## Current State

- `sig` field is hardcoded as `"placeholder_signature"` (line ~65)
- Public key derivation uses SHA256 hash instead of proper secp256k1 key derivation
- Comments explicitly mark these as placeholders

## What's Needed

- Add a `secp256k1` (or `k256`) crate dependency for proper elliptic curve signing
- Derive the public key from the Nostr secret key (nsec)
- Sign events per [NIP-01](https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md) specification
- Compute the event ID as SHA256 of the serialized event

## Impact

Without real signing, published Nostr events will be rejected by relays. This is the only functional gap blocking full Nostr support.
